commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The protected maintainer label, security-sensitive fail-closed behavior, compatibility risk, and missing real behavior proof require human maintainer handling rather than an automated fix PR.; Cleared: The diff hardens a Discord authorization boundary and adds no dependency, workflow, package, secret, or supply-chain changes; no concrete security regression was found. (sha=48959a485d65440e7078d17a2aa8a56fe8240278)
commands.review_summary:
The PR centralizes Discord privileged guild-admin action detection and requires trusted Discord requester identity for those actions across channel origins while preserving read-only guild lookups.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +13. Total +24 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection. Current main lets non-Discord-origin privileged Discord guild-admin actions bypass the trusted-sender guard, drops the requester identity, and reaches runtime code that skips permission checks when senderUserId is absent.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The protected maintainer label, security-sensitive fail-closed behavior, compatibility risk, and missing real behavior proof require human maintainer handling rather than an automated fix PR.

target.title: fix(discord): block cross-provider guild admin actions
target.body:
Summary

Fixes Discord guild-admin message action authorization so privileged Discord admin actions require a trusted Discord requester identity even when the action is invoked from another channel provider.

Changes

- Centralize the Discord privileged guild-admin action list in a shared helper.
- Require trusted requester identity for those Discord actions regardless of current provider.
- Reject non-Discord origins in the Discord guild-admin handler instead of dropping the requester identity and continuing through a no-sender path.
- Keep read-only Discord guild lookups available from non-Discord requesters.

Validation

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts discord/src/actions/handle-action.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts discord/src --reporter=dot`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Cross-provider attempts to run privileged Discord guild-admin message actions are rejected unless they originate with a trusted Discord sender identity.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `747`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts discord/src --reporter=dot`.
Evidence after fix: Discord extension lane passed with 158 test files and 1763 tests.
Observed result after fix: The regression tests now require trusted requester identity across origins and reject non-Discord requester IDs for privileged Discord guild-admin actions while preserving read-only guild lookups.
What was not tested: No live Discord API calls were run; validation used the existing Discord extension unit/runtime boundaries.

Notes

- `CHANGELOG.md` was not updated.
- `USER.md` was updated locally with the requested worklog and intentionally left out of the commit.
target.author: eleqtrizit

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
